Title: A linear nonribosomal octapeptide from Fusarium graminearum facilitates cell-to-cell invasion of graminaceous wheat

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Virulence factor enabling cell-to-cell invasion in wheat
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full wheat virulence and cell-to-cell invasion but not for fusaoctaxin A biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fgm5 deletion caused severe reduction in wheat coleoptile and spike infection, resembling fg3_54 cluster deletion. 2. Mutant hyphae were blocked near inoculation sites, but co-inoculation with wild type rescued spread, suggesting dependence on diffusible cluster product(s). 3. In the fgm4-overexpression background, deleting fgm5 did not block fusaoctaxin A production, excluding it from direct biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted gene deletion; fluorescent infection imaging; co-inoculation rescue assay; LC-MS in fgm4-overexpression background
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: wheat infection in planta; co-inoculation with wild type
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE89867; infection-related transcript evidence also supported in GEO: GSE117934
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No detectable in vitro growth defect under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Known previously to function in fungal virulence and azole tolerance; likely involved in transport/export or self-protection rather than peptide assembly.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible target for suppressing Fusarium virulence and/or chemical tolerance.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to full wheat virulence but is not required for fusaoctaxin A production.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fgm1 deletion reduced lesion size on wheat coleoptiles and reduced spike infection symptoms. 2. Deleting fgm1 in the fgm4-overexpression background did not affect fusaoctaxin A production. 3. Therefore fgm1 contributes to virulence through a cluster-associated function other than core octapeptide assembly.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted gene deletion; plant infection assays; LC-MS in fgm4-overexpression background
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE89867; infection-related transcript evidence also supported in GEO: GSE117934
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious in vitro growth defect reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite annotation as a P450 monooxygenase, no direct biosynthetic role in fusaoctaxin A production was supported.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential secondary target to attenuate virulence.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to full wheat virulence but is not required for fusaoctaxin A biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fgm9 deletion reduced lesion size and blight symptoms in wheat. 2. In the fgm4-overexpression background, deleting fgm9 did not abolish fusaoctaxin A production. 3. The gene therefore supports cluster-associated virulence but is dispensable for direct octapeptide synthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted gene deletion; infection assays; LC-MS in fgm4-overexpression background
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE89867; infection-related transcript evidence also supported in GEO: GSE117934
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious in vitro growth defect reported
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Annotated by similarity to fungal short-chain alcohol dehydrogenase; direct metabolic role unresolved.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible auxiliary target for reducing virulence.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to wheat virulence but is not required for fusaoctaxin A biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Previous and current evidence showed fgm2 deletion reduces wheat coleoptile lesion size. 2. In the fgm4-overexpression background, deleting fgm2 did not affect fusaoctaxin A production. 3. fgm2 is therefore a virulence-associated cluster gene but not part of the core NRPS biosynthetic machinery for fusaoctaxin A.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted gene deletion; infection assays; LC-MS in fgm4-overexpression background
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE89867; infection-related transcript evidence also supported in GEO: GSE117934
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No detectable in vitro growth defect under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein shows similarity to a fungal polysaccharide deacetylase; exact role in virulence remains unresolved.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for disease control.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: nonribosomal octapeptide assembly line elongation/termination module
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: peptidyl intermediates and amino acid extender units including L-Ala, L-Ile, L-Ser, L-Val and L-Leu
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as the seven-module elongation and terminal reductive-release NRPS that sequentially incorporates seven residues into the growing peptide, epimerizes modules M3-M7 to D-amino acids, and reductively releases the final linear octapeptide alcohol.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fungal virulence factor enabling cell-to-cell invasion of wheat tissues and suppression of host defense responses such as callose deposition/papillae formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for fusaoctaxin A production and promotes wheat infection/cell-to-cell invasion.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nrps5 is one of two core structural genes in the fg3_54 cluster and is strongly induced during wheat infection but not under standard in vitro culture. 2. Deletion of nrps5 caused drastic reduction of wheat coleoptile lesion size and reduced virulence, similar to fg3_54 cluster deletion. 3. In the fgm4-overexpression background, deletion of nrps5 abolished fusaoctaxin A production, confirming nrps5 is essential for octapeptide biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted gene deletion; overexpression of fgm4 to activate cluster; LC-MS/LC-MS-MS metabolite profiling; structural elucidation of product; plant infection assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Barely detected in vitro; greatly upregulated during wheat infection around 64 hpi and reduced after 72 hpi
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: wheat infection in planta; constitutive fgm4 overexpression for in vitro activation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE89867; infection-related transcript evidence also supported in GEO: GSE117934
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NRPS megaenzyme with condensation, adenylation, thiolation, epimerization and reductase domains
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key structural enzyme for target-product formation, but rate-limiting not explicitly established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NRPS5 is the only F. graminearum NRPS reported here with a terminal reductase domain producing a linear peptide alcohol; N-terminal partial deletion mutant showed severe virulence loss.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target to block fusaoctaxin A biosynthesis and reduce Fusarium virulence in wheat.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: GABA-loaded peptide intermediate leading to fusaoctaxin A
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts as a loading-module NRPS di-domain protein that likely activates and loads the starter unit GABA for transfer into the NRPS5 assembly line during fusaoctaxin A biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fungal virulence factor enabling cell-to-cell invasion of wheat tissues and suppression of host defense responses.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for fusaoctaxin A production and strongly contributes to wheat virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nrps9 is a core fg3_54 structural gene induced during wheat infection but poorly expressed in vitro. 2. Deletion of nrps9 reduced lesion size and virulence on wheat. 3. In the fgm4-overexpression background, deletion of nrps9 abolished fusaoctaxin A production, supporting its role as the starter-unit loading NRPS in octapeptide biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted gene deletion; fgm4 overexpression activation; LC-MS metabolite profiling; infection phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Barely detected in vitro; strongly induced during wheat infection around 64 hpi
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: wheat infection in planta; constitutive fgm4 overexpression for in vitro activation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE89867; infection-related transcript evidence also supported in GEO: GSE117934
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NRPS di-domain protein with adenylation and thiolation domains
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key initiating structural enzyme, not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted to serve as a loading module for starter-unit incorporation; likely uses GABA as starter unit.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target to block fusaoctaxin A biosynthesis and virulence.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: bANK family protein with basic DNA-binding domain and ankyrin-like repeats
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: biosynthesis of the virulence-associated nonribosomal peptide fusaoctaxin A
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: nrps5; nrps9; other fg3_54 cluster genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates transcription of fg3_54 cluster genes selectively, including nrps5 and nrps9
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Pathway-specific activator required to turn on the silent fg3_54 cluster and enable fusaoctaxin A production.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Virulence factor that facilitates cell-to-cell invasion of wheat and suppresses host defense responses.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes fusaoctaxin A biosynthesis and wheat infection by activating fg3_54 cluster expression.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fgm4 mutants phenocopied major virulence defects of fg3_54 and nrps5 mutants in wheat. 2. FGM4 localized to nuclei and activated transcription in yeast, supporting regulator function. 3. Constitutive overexpression of fgm4 selectively induced fg3_54 cluster expression in vitro and enabled production of fusaoctaxin A.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion; complementation; constitutive overexpression; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR/semi-qRT-PCR; yeast transcription activation assay; fluorescent protein localization; metabolite profiling; plant infection assays
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Normally associated with in planta fg3_54 activation; overexpression with pEF1 drives in vitro expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: wheat infection in planta; constitutive pEF1-driven overexpression in vitro
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GEO: GSE89867; infection-related transcript evidence also supported in GEO: GSE117934
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No detectable in vitro growth defect reported for deletion mutants under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FGM4 was identified after reanalysis as a bANK-like regulator rather than an enzyme; FgLaeA overexpression did not induce fg3_54 cluster genes.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting FGM4-regulated pathway activation could suppress fusaoctaxin A production and reduce wheat infection.
